citation.anchor: There are at least 8 instruction and 8 data TR slots implemented on all processor models

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with PSR.ic zero during all ITR.D insertions
Eight nonoverlapping 4-KiB virtual pages and eight distinct physical data pages use one RID
inputs: Slots 0 through 7 in increasing installation order
Eight distinct present readable translation images
Reverse-order translated loads after data serialization
expected_results: Every DTR slot retains its independently selected mapping
Reverse lookup returns all eight exact physical data values
No slot aliases or displaces another nonoverlapping slot
allowed_results: No diagnostic interruption remains after the eight translated loads
required_unchanged_state: ITR bank and unrelated architectural state
cases.committed_prefix: All eight stopped ITR.D insertions commit before one data serialization and the reverse lookup traversal
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for legal nonoverlapping DTR insertion or lookup

oracle.derivation: The slot permutation, virtual pages, physical pages, data values, reverse access order, and expected translations are test-owned literals derived from the cited eight-slot fully-associative contract.

limitations: Same-stream overlapping TR insertion and its required Machine Check abort are a separate unclosed contract

citation.anchor: Translation registers support all implemented page sizes and must be implemented in a single-level fully-associative array

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with PSR.ic zero during all ITR.I insertions
Eight nonoverlapping executable mappings use one RID; slot zero also maps the alternate-ITLB vector
inputs: Slots 0 through 7 in increasing installation order
A translated branch chain that visits one marker bundle through each final slot
RFI supplies the required instruction serialization before the first fetch
expected_results: Every ITR slot supplies its independently selected instruction mapping
All eight marker GRs receive their slot-specific literal
The translated chain reaches its final virtual sentinel without a miss
allowed_results: No diagnostic interruption remains after the translated fetch chain
required_unchanged_state: Mapped instruction bundles and unrelated architectural state
cases.committed_prefix: All stopped ITR.I insertions commit before RFI; every mapped marker and branch commits in chain order
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for legal nonoverlapping ITR insertion or fetch

result_observations: final virtual IP and exception state

oracle.derivation: The slot sequence, virtual-to-physical code layout, branch chain, marker literals, and final IP are test-owned values derived from the cited eight-slot fully-associative contract.

limitations: Same-stream overlapping TR insertion and its required Machine Check abort are a separate unclosed contract

citation.anchor: The translation previously contained in the TR slot specified by GR r3 is not necessarily purged from the processor's TLBs and may remain as a TC entry

preconditions: Each eight-slot bank first contains nonoverlapping valid translations
The replacement virtual page does not overlap any existing translation in its stream
inputs: A second ITR.D write to occupied DTR slot 5
A second ITR.I write to occupied ITR slot 3
Post-serialization lookup through every final slot mapping
expected_results: The selected occupied slot accepts the new nonoverlapping mapping
All seven unselected slots in each bank remain usable
The test makes no assertion about an implementation-permitted cached copy of the old mapping
allowed_results: The overwritten old translation may be absent or may remain as a TC entry; it is not referenced by this contract
permitted_state_changes: Implementation-dependent TC copies of overwritten mappings
Result GRs and translation lookup cache
cases.committed_prefix: The initial eight-slot images commit before the selected nonoverlapping overwrite; serialized final traversals observe the replacement and every unselected slot
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for a legal nonoverlapping occupied-slot replacement

oracle.derivation: The overwritten slots, nonoverlapping replacement pages, final slot traversals, and refusal to require old-TC eviction are independently selected from the cited ITR semantics.

limitations: Software requiring guaranteed removal of the old translation must issue PTR before overwrite
Overlapping same-stream insertion requires Machine Check handling and is not claimed here

citation.anchor: translation registers as well as cached translation register copies that may be contained in the respective translation caches

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with one selected 4-KiB RID/range
Fresh-process cases create dynamic and pinned same-stream entries plus legal same-address opposite-stream mappings
inputs: PTR.D over a DTC entry and over a DTR entry
PTR.I over an ITC entry and over an ITR entry
PTR.I with a same-address DTR and PTR.D with a same-address ITR
expected_results: Each PTR form removes overlapping same-stream TC and TR mappings
The next same-stream access or fetch misses after serialization
The same-address opposite-stream mapping remains usable
allowed_results: A PTR may overpurge additional entries within its own translation cache
No opposite-stream TR may be removed
permitted_state_changes: Overlapping same-stream TR and TC validity
expected_faults: Alternate Instruction TLB Miss after purged instruction mappings
Alternate Data TLB Miss after purged data mappings
cases.committed_prefix: Each insertion and PTR commits before its required serialization; the post-purge same-stream reference faults without committing while the opposite-stream probe completes
cases.restart_action: The lifecycle cases terminate at the expected miss vector; isolation cases require no restart

result_observations: exact post-purge miss vector and IIP
opposite-stream loaded value or instruction marker

oracle.derivation: The dynamic/pinned entry forms, same-address opposite-stream pairings, purge ranges, expected miss identities, and survivor values are test-owned literals derived from the cited PTR stream rules.

limitations: PTR overlap with a same-stream TR is legal and covered; PTC or insert overlap with a TR and their required Machine Check abort are separate unclosed contracts
